guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines: Oral and Dental (TMD/dental infection)
RACGP guidance on giant cell arteritis
intentional_test_features: Red herrings: acute trismus with facial pain and headache mimics a deep-space dental abscess, giant cell arteritis and tetanus.
Important-not-to-miss: deep-space dental infection/airway (Ludwig's-type), GCA and jaw dislocation must be screened; trismus risks dehydration.
Anchoring: 'known fibromyalgia/TMD' should not close off infection/GCA.
Commission trap: home diazepam and de-novo amitriptyline do not translate to remote initiation.
Telehealth limits: intra-oral/masseter/temporal-artery examination and jaw-opening measurement cannot be done over chat.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date)
